source: OIC ERP Accounting Module V2 — Infrastructure, Transaction Types & Report Integrity (gap analysis against full accounting spec)

features.title: COA Expansion — Add All Missing System Accounts to SYS_ACCOUNTS & Seed
acceptance_criteria: Add these accounts to SYS_ACCOUNTS in lib/gl-accounts.ts with correct codes: ALLOWANCE_DOUBTFUL='1210', GR_IR_CLEARING='2150', SALARY_PAYABLE='2200', MFG_OVERHEAD_APPLIED='2210', PPH21_PAYABLE='2310', PPH23_PAYABLE='2315', BPJS_TK_PAYABLE='2320', BPJS_KES_PAYABLE='2330', UNEARNED_REVENUE='2400', SERVICE_REVENUE='4200', OTHER_INCOME='4300', INTEREST_INCOME='4400', SALARY_EXPENSE='6100', BAD_DEBT_EXPENSE='6500', BANK_CHARGES='7200'
Add all new accounts to SYSTEM_ACCOUNT_DEFS[] with correct AccountType (ASSET/LIABILITY/EQUITY/REVENUE/EXPENSE) and Indonesian names
Add all new accounts to prisma/seed-gl.ts so fresh databases get them
ALLOWANCE_DOUBTFUL (1210) type=ASSET (contra-asset with CREDIT normal balance — note in name: 'Cadangan Kerugian Piutang')
GR_IR_CLEARING (2150) type=LIABILITY (name: 'Barang Diterima / Faktur Belum Diterima')
All payroll liability accounts (2200, 2310, 2315, 2320, 2330) type=LIABILITY
UNEARNED_REVENUE (2400) type=LIABILITY (name: 'Pendapatan Diterima Dimuka')
SERVICE_REVENUE (4200) type=REVENUE, OTHER_INCOME (4300) type=REVENUE, INTEREST_INCOME (4400) type=REVENUE
SALARY_EXPENSE (6100) type=EXPENSE, BAD_DEBT_EXPENSE (6500) type=EXPENSE, BANK_CHARGES (7200) type=EXPENSE
npx vitest run passes (no regressions)
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: Product Item Master Accounting Fields — cogsAccountId, inventoryAccountId, incomeAccountId
acceptance_criteria: Add to Product model in prisma/schema.prisma: cogsAccountId (String? @db.Uuid, FK to GLAccount), inventoryAccountId (String? @db.Uuid, FK to GLAccount), incomeAccountId (String? @db.Uuid, FK to GLAccount), purchaseAccountId (String? @db.Uuid, FK to GLAccount)
Add relations: cogsAccount, inventoryAccount, incomeAccount, purchaseAccount — all optional relations to GLAccount
Add reverse relations on GLAccount model (e.g. productsAsCogs, productsAsInventory, etc.)
Create Prisma migration with: npx prisma migrate dev --name add_product_accounting_fields
npx prisma generate succeeds and Prisma client has the new fields
Default values: NULL for all four fields (existing products keep working without accounting links)
npx vitest run passes (no regressions)
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: GLAccount Control Account Flag & Direct Posting Restriction
acceptance_criteria: Add to GLAccount model in prisma/schema.prisma: isControlAccount (Boolean @default(false)), allowDirectPosting (Boolean @default(true))
Create Prisma migration with: npx prisma migrate dev --name add_gl_control_account_flags
In seed-gl.ts or a new migration: set isControlAccount=true and allowDirectPosting=false for accounts 1200 (AR), 2000 (AP), 1300 (Inventory)

In postJournalEntry() in finance-gl.ts: before creating journal lines, check each account's allowDirectPosting flag. If false AND the source_document_type is 'MANUAL' (manual journal entry), reject with error 'Akun kontrol [code] tidak boleh diposting langsung — gunakan modul AR/AP/Inventory'
System-generated entries (from invoice, payment, GRN etc.) must STILL be allowed to post to control accounts — only MANUAL journal entries are blocked
npx vitest run passes (no regressions)
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: Fiscal Period Enforcement — Block Posting to Closed Periods
acceptance_criteria: In postJournalEntry() in finance-gl.ts: before creating the journal entry, look up the FiscalPeriod for the journal date's year/month
If a FiscalPeriod exists AND isClosed=true, reject with error: 'Periode [month/year] sudah ditutup — tidak dapat membuat jurnal'
If NO FiscalPeriod exists for that month, ALLOW the posting (auto-create period or simply don't block — periods are optional)
This validation applies to ALL journal entries (manual and system-generated) — you cannot post to a closed period
Add a Vitest test: mock a closed fiscal period, attempt postJournalEntry(), verify it throws the expected error
npx vitest run passes
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: GR/IR Clearing Account Flow — Two-Step Inventory Receipt
acceptance_criteria: SYS_ACCOUNTS.GR_IR_CLEARING ('2150') exists (from ACCT2-001)
In GRN receipt GL posting (lib/actions/inventory-gl.ts or wherever PO_RECEIVE is handled): change journal from DR Inventory CR AP → DR Inventory (1300) CR GR/IR Clearing (2150)

When vendor bill (INV_IN) is posted for a PO-linked invoice in finance-invoices.ts or finance-ap.ts: journal should include DR GR/IR Clearing (2150) instead of DR Inventory when goods were already received via GRN
Full AP bill journal for PO-received goods: DR GR/IR Clearing (2150) [goods cost], DR PPN Masukan (1330) [tax], CR AP (2000) [total]
For NON-PO bills (direct expense purchase), existing flow remains: DR Expense/Inventory, DR PPN, CR AP
Net effect: after both GRN receipt and bill posting, GR/IR Clearing (2150) balance returns to zero for that transaction
npx vitest run passes (no regressions)
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: COGS Trigger on Sales Delivery — Auto-Post HPP from Item Master Config
acceptance_criteria: When a sales invoice (INV_OUT) with STOCK_ITEM products is posted via moveInvoiceToSent(), automatically generate a COGS journal entry
COGS journal: DR COGS account (product.cogsAccountId or fallback SYS_ACCOUNTS.COGS '5000'), CR Inventory account (product.inventoryAccountId or fallback SYS_ACCOUNTS.INVENTORY_ASSET '1300')
COGS amount per item = quantity × product.costPrice (WAC — Weighted Average Cost)
If product has no costPrice or costPrice=0, log a warning but do NOT block invoice posting (COGS entry skipped for that item with console.warn)
If product.cogsAccountId is set, use it; otherwise fallback to SYS_ACCOUNTS.COGS
If product.inventoryAccountId is set, use it; otherwise fallback to SYS_ACCOUNTS.INVENTORY_ASSET
The COGS journal entry is SEPARATE from the AR/Revenue entry (two journal entries per sales invoice with stock items)
COGS journal has source_document_type: 'COGS_RECOGNITION' for audit trail
npx vitest run passes
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: Bad Debt Write-Off — Direct Write-Off & Allowance Method
acceptance_criteria: New function writeOffBadDebt() in lib/actions/finance-ar.ts
Parameters: invoiceId, method ('DIRECT' | 'ALLOWANCE'), amount (partial write-off supported)
DIRECT method journal: DR Bad Debt Expense (SYS_ACCOUNTS.BAD_DEBT_EXPENSE '6500'), CR AR (SYS_ACCOUNTS.AR '1200')
ALLOWANCE method Step 1 — provisionBadDebt(amount): DR Bad Debt Expense (6500), CR Allowance for Doubtful Debts (SYS_ACCOUNTS.ALLOWANCE_DOUBTFUL '1210')
ALLOWANCE method Step 2 — writeOffBadDebt with method='ALLOWANCE': DR Allowance for Doubtful Debts (1210), CR AR (1200) — does NOT hit P&L again
Updates invoice status to VOID or reduces balanceDue by write-off amount
Removes written-off amount from AR Aging report
Uses ensureSystemAccounts() before posting
Journal entry has source_document_type: 'BAD_DEBT_WRITEOFF'
npx vitest run passes
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: WHT/PPh 23 on Vendor Payments — Withholding Tax Deduction
acceptance_criteria: Extend recordVendorPayment() in finance-ap.ts to accept optional whtAmount (withholding tax amount) and whtRate (e.g. 0.02 for 2% PPh 23)
When whtAmount > 0, journal changes from: DR AP, CR Bank → DR AP (2000) [gross], CR Bank (1110) [net = gross - wht], CR PPh 23 Payable (SYS_ACCOUNTS.PPH23_PAYABLE '2315') [whtAmount]
Invoice balanceDue decreases by GROSS amount (wht is applied against the invoice total)
Payment record stores whtAmount for tax reporting
When whtAmount is 0 or not provided, existing flow is unchanged (backwards compatible)
Uses ensureSystemAccounts() before posting
npx vitest run passes
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: AR Payment Bank Charges — Deduction Handling on Customer Payments
acceptance_criteria: Extend recordARPayment() in finance-ar.ts to accept optional bankChargeAmount parameter
When bankChargeAmount > 0, journal: DR Bank (1110) [amountReceived - bankCharge], DR Bank Charges (SYS_ACCOUNTS.BANK_CHARGES '7200') [bankChargeAmount], CR AR (1200) [amountReceived]
Invoice balanceDue decreases by full amountReceived (bank charge is company's expense, not customer's)
When bankChargeAmount is 0 or not provided, existing flow unchanged (backwards compatible)
Bank charge amount flows to P&L as expense (account 7200)
Uses ensureSystemAccounts() before posting
npx vitest run passes
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: Payroll GL Account Configuration — SYS_ACCOUNTS Instead of Keyword Matching
acceptance_criteria: In app/actions/hcm.ts payroll GL posting functions: replace keyword-matching account lookups (searching for 'gaji/salary/payroll') with direct SYS_ACCOUNTS references

Payroll calculation journal: DR SYS_ACCOUNTS.SALARY_EXPENSE (6100), CR SYS_ACCOUNTS.SALARY_PAYABLE (2200) [net], CR SYS_ACCOUNTS.PPH21_PAYABLE (2310) [tax], CR SYS_ACCOUNTS.BPJS_TK_PAYABLE (2320) [bpjs tk], CR SYS_ACCOUNTS.BPJS_KES_PAYABLE (2330) [bpjs kes]
Salary disbursement journal: DR SYS_ACCOUNTS.SALARY_PAYABLE (2200), CR Bank account (SYS_ACCOUNTS.BANK_BCA or specified)
Import SYS_ACCOUNTS from lib/gl-accounts.ts and call ensureSystemAccounts() before posting
Remove or deprecate the keyword-matching fallback logic (searching for 'gaji', 'salary', etc.)
npx vitest run passes
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: Bank Reconciliation Auto GL — Post Charges & Interest During Recon
acceptance_criteria: When a BankReconciliationItem is marked as 'bank_charge' (or type=BANK_CHARGE), auto-create journal: DR SYS_ACCOUNTS.BANK_CHARGES (7200), CR Bank GL account [bankAccount from reconciliation]
When a BankReconciliationItem is marked as 'interest_income' (or type=INTEREST_INCOME), auto-create journal: DR Bank GL account, CR SYS_ACCOUNTS.INTEREST_INCOME (4400)
Journal entries created during reconciliation finalization (when status moves to completed/posted)
Each auto-GL entry has source_document_type: 'BANK_RECONCILIATION'
Uses ensureSystemAccounts() before posting
npx vitest run passes
npx tsc --noEmit passes

features.title: Immutable Posted Entries & Journal Reversal Mechanism
acceptance_criteria: In finance-gl.ts: add guard to prevent updating or deleting journal entries where status=POSTED — throw error 'Jurnal yang sudah diposting tidak dapat diubah — buat jurnal balik'

New function reverseJournalEntry(journalEntryId) in finance-gl.ts that: creates a new journal entry with all debit/credit lines swapped (debits become credits, credits become debits), sets source_document_type to 'REVERSAL', sets description to 'Pembalikan: [original description]', stores reference to original entry
Reversal entry is auto-posted (status=POSTED) immediately
After reversal, original entry is marked with a flag (e.g. isReversed=true or reversedById pointing to reversal entry) — but NOT deleted or voided
Net GL impact of original + reversal = zero for all affected accounts
npx vitest run passes
npx tsc --noEmit passes
